# @vllnt/convex-wallet

> Consumable balances and economy ledger with atomic spend and lazy regen, as a Convex component

`@vllnt/convex-wallet` is a publishable Convex component. It targets `convex@^1.36.1`.
It holds per-subject balances of any consumable currency (coins, energy, gems, credits) in two sandboxed tables - `balances` (one per `(subjectRef, currency)`, with `amount`, `lastRegenAt`, and lifetime counters) and `ledger` (a signed-`delta` row per change, with an optional `idempotencyKey` indexed per `(subjectRef, currency, idempotencyKey)`). Spend and transfer are atomic within the Convex mutation transaction, so balances never go negative and grants never double-credit. Every value-moving call rejects non-finite or non-positive amounts (`INVALID_AMOUNT`), so a negative `spend` can never mint funds; `transfer` rejects self-transfer (`SELF_TRANSFER`); a rejected spend/transfer returns a typed `code` (`INSUFFICIENT` | `SELF_TRANSFER`). Energy-style currencies regenerate lazily on read via a pure `applyRegen` helper (gain `amount` per `intervalMs` up to `cap`, monotonic against a backwards/stale clock); **time is server-sourced** - every handler reads `Date.now()` itself, and the `Wallet` client injects each currency's `regen` rule and optional `max` ceiling. An internal hourly cron prunes old ledger rows and expires stale idempotency keys (idempotent / at-least-once). The host owns identity, meaning, and payment confirmation - it verifies any IAP / Stripe purchase, then calls `grant`. The component never processes payments.
